title: [FIRRTL/IMConstantProp] Reach pessimistic fixpoint if one of operands is an invalid value
body:
Currently, if the lattice value of one of operands is an invalid value and folding fails, we mark the current operation as overdefined immediately. 
It causes to reach some pessimistic fixpoint even though there is a room for constant propagation. For example,
``` mlir
// circt-opt -pass-pipeline='firrtl.circuit(firrtl-imconstprop)'
firrtl.circuit "PessimisticFixpoint"   {
  firrtl.module @PessimisticFixpoint(in %clock: !firrtl.clock, in %a: !firrtl.uint<1>, out %b: !firrtl.uint<2>) {
    %r1 = firrtl.reg %clock  : !firrtl.uint<1>
    %r2 = firrtl.reg %clock  : !firrtl.uint<1>
    %r3 = firrtl.reg %clock  : !firrtl.uint<1>
    %r4 = firrtl.reg %clock  : !firrtl.uint<1>


    %c1_ui1 = firrtl.constant 1 : !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.connect %r1, %c1_ui1 : !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.connect %r3, %c1_ui1 : !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.connect %r2, %r1 : !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.connect %r4, %r3 : !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>
    %0 = firrtl.add %r2, %r4 : (!firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>) -> !firrtl.uint<2>
    firrtl.connect %b, %0 : !firrtl.uint<2>, !firrtl.uint<2>
  }
}
```
currently becomes
``` mlir
firrtl.circuit "PessimisticFixpoint"   {
  firrtl.module @PessimisticFixpoint(in %clock: !firrtl.clock, in %a: !firrtl.uint<1>, out %b: !firrtl.uint<2>) {
      %c1_ui1 = firrtl.constant 1 : !firrtl.uint<1>
      %c1_ui1_0 = firrtl.constant 1 : !firrtl.uint<1>
      %0 = firrtl.add %c1_ui1, %c1_ui1_0 : (!firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>) -> !firrtl.uint<2>
      firrtl.connect %b, %0 : !firrtl.uint<2>, !firrtl.uint<2>
  }
}
``` 
In this example, we visit `%0 = firrtl.add %r2, %r4` when the lattice of `%4` is invalid value, and therefore `%0` is marked as overdefined.
